mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand conditions that drive corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60d returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts equity earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 150k signals strong labor market performance that supports consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,400k signals rising demand for residential construction that boosts related sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1,400k.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2% and an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.5% signals a balanced monetary policy that stabilizes equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 60d returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
